title: alnus alnobetula subsp. alnobetula
intro: Alnus alnobetula subsp. alnobetula, also known as mountain alder or green alder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to subarctic and alpine regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, where its bark, leaves, and roots are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Alnus alnobetula subsp. alnobetula include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of Alnus alnobetula subsp. alnobetula include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often made from the bark, leaves, or roots of the plant, which are used to support respiratory health, reduce pain and inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
